CamNet Inc · Claude Integration · Status & Roadmap

CamNet Claude Integration: Status and Roadmap

Follow-up to the July 22 walkthrough call. This covers what has been built so far, where each tool stands, and what is needed to unblock each thread.

Prepared for
CamNet
Scope
Claude integration in Salesforce
Status
Live in production, beta access
Date
2026-07-22
01What's Live Today 02Quoting Tools 03Recently Completed 04Roadmap & What Unblocks It
01

What's Live Today

One app inside Salesforce, several tools. Access is limited during beta.

The Claude Copilot app

The app lives in the App Launcher (the grid icon, top left in Salesforce) as Claude Copilot. Everything below lives in that one app, as tabs along the top. Under the hood it is custom code inside Salesforce that talks to Claude over a secured connection, using a set of pre-built tools that run real queries against CamNet's own data. The question-answering tools are read-only, and the builders only create records when a button is clicked. The important consequence: answers come from live data at the moment of the question, and figures are checked against the source before they are shown.

Opening the Claude Copilot app from the App Launcher.

Chat in use: a conversation replayed from history, with the summary, verified figures, and clickable account links.

TabWhat it doesState
ChatOpen-ended questions about the business in plain English ("What's our total revenue this year?", "Which accounts have we lost money on?"). Answers include a short summary, tables, and clickable links that open the real Salesforce records.Working
Report BuilderA user describes a report in plain English, refines it conversationally ("only include opportunities over 50k"), clicks once, and a real Salesforce report is created automatically.Working
Quote BuilderA rep describes a job in chat and gets a priced draft quote built from CamNet's own catalog, cost records, and past work. Covered fully in section 02.Working
Bundle BuilderAssembles a product bundle for a typical job type (for example a 2-door access control install) from the catalog.Working
Read (wiki)A knowledge base of 691 pages that grows automatically from real conversations, searchable and browsable.Working
Flow BuilderWill draft Salesforce automations from a plain-English description. Anything it creates arrives switched OFF, so it can never change org behavior on its own.In progress
Materials / Account HealthFocused analytics views (material spend, account margin and activity health).Working

Who has access, and how

Access is granted through Salesforce permission sets (named bundles of permissions assigned per person). Today the app is limited to three users: Mollie, Andy, and the Great Blue admin account. Conversation history is private per person: each user only ever sees their own conversations.

Blocked on sign-off

Wider rollout to the rest of the team is waiting on CamNet's go-ahead.

02

Quoting Tools

Quoting speed was named the top priority on the call. Here is the full picture.

Quote Builder (live in the Claude Copilot app)

For quotes that start from what CamNet already knows: a rep describes the job in chat, the tool asks a few clarifying questions, then produces an editable draft with products, quantities, unit prices, and the cost and margin on every line. Costs come from CamNet's own cost records, and a rep can correct a cost right on the draft before creating the quote. One click then creates a real CPQ quote in Salesforce. This exists today and works end to end. It was highlighted on the call but not demoed for time.

Quote Builder in use: catalog checks, a clarifying question on a duplicate-priced part, and the draft quote taking shape.

Bid Quoter (in progress, needs more test data)

For the bid-board estimating process: an estimator uploads a specification PDF, and the system reads the products section, pulls out each requirement, matches it against CamNet's product catalog, prices matched lines from bids CamNet has actually won, and produces a draft quote for review. What takes half a day by hand comes back in about a minute, at a processing cost of under a dollar for a full spec book.

Opening the Bid Quoting app from the App Launcher.

The Bid Quoting app: a draft built from a real spec section, with pricing pulled from won bids.

Where it stands: it works end to end, proven on the Zuni spec book, the one project it has been run against start to finish. What it needs now is exposure to more real bid packages so the matching tunes to CamNet's catalog across different projects and spec-writing styles. That is the test-data request in section 04, and it is the single biggest thing that moves both the Bid Quoter and the diagram counter forward.

Diagram counter (in progress, needs more test data)

The drawings answer the question the spec text cannot: how many devices, room by room. Today, draft quantities start from what the spec text states and default to one, with real counts and cable footage entered by hand during review. That manual step is what the counter replaces. We tested whether Claude can read a drawing sheet's own legend and count devices, across drawing sets from three different design firms. Result: it is accurate enough to be a strong first pass that a person reviews, and not yet trustworthy enough to run blind (it can miscount repeated symbols and once invented a device from a door-swing mark). Its counts would flow straight into the Bid Quoter draft, pre-filling quantities. The remaining work needs the same thing the Bid Quoter does: more real projects, in this case more drawing sets to test the counting against.

How it adds up

Spec text tells the quote WHAT products. Drawings tell it HOW MANY. Both tools are unblocked by the same thing, more real bid packages, and together they draft the whole bid for an estimator to review rather than build.

03

Recently Completed

The last few weeks in brief.

  • Privacy lockdown (the rollout gate). Conversation history is strictly per-user, verified with dedicated tests and confirmed live July 22. This was the requirement set before wider rollout, and it is closed.
  • Permission gating on financial data. The tools that answer company-wide financial questions (revenue, margins, profitability) are restricted to authorized users and enforced on the server, so access rules hold even against creative prompting. Neutral tools, including a personal hours lookup, stay open to every user of the app.
  • Quote cost accuracy. Fixed a bug where a product with multiple cost records could pick up a stale cost (which once showed a wildly wrong margin). Every quoting path now always uses the newest cost record, and reps can correct costs inline on the draft.
  • Report Builder and Quote Builder polish. The plain-English-to-spec interview flow, clickable record links, "no cost loaded" notes where catalog data is missing, and a cleaner progress indicator while the AI works.
  • Bid Quoter reliability and cost. Independent review of the pipeline, cost of a full-book run cut by roughly 75 percent, caching so a re-uploaded document skips the expensive reading step and returns the same draft every time, and a pre-upload cost estimate with a confirm step so processing spend is always visible before it happens.
04

Roadmap & What Unblocks It

What moves each thread forward, in priority order.

1 · Test data for the Bid Quoter and diagram counter

What we have tested with so far, in full:

ItemTypeUsed for
Zuni MS/HS project: Division 27/28 spec book, section extracts, and technology drawingsPDFThe spec book (~220 pages) is the Bid Quoter's main test document, used for every end-to-end run. The drawings fed the device-counting study
Mescalero Indian Hospital packagePDF, spec section extractIts spec section was tested and yielded few line items, a useful finding: narrative-style specs extract far less than itemized ones. Its won quote also fed matcher cross-checks
Watson HS packagePDF, project manual and drawingsThe drawings fed the device-counting study. The full manual became testable once large-document ingest landed
Vladem Contemporary full bid setPDF, full packageUsed in the device-counting study
Won-quote exports for the Zuni and Mescalero projectsSpreadsheets (CSV)Ground truth for checking the matcher picks the right products

What is needed to unblock this: more real bid packages, ideally several from different general contractors and design firms. Every bid document held today is PDF. If bids arrive in other formats (DWG drawing files, for example), representative samples are needed to assess what supporting them takes.

2 · Data quality

Output quality rides on data quality. The two that matter most: the product catalog (current products present, consistent naming, manufacturer recorded) and current price and cost records (the Quote Builder shows margin wherever a cost record exists, and shows a "no cost loaded" note where one does not). A scrubbed price list directly widens how much of a draft quote comes back priced. Great Blue can produce a gap list from the system's side (for example, products most quoted but missing costs) whenever useful to whoever owns the cleanup.

3 · Rollout decisions

  • Privacy-gate sign-off (the fix is live and verified), which starts wider rollout.
  • The order and audience for the next access wave (the permission-set model makes this per-person).

4 · Cadence

Per the call: hands-on exploration of the app this week, internal alignment on rollout, then short 10-to-15-minute check-ins as needed.

Prepared 2026-07-22 · Great Blue · CamNet Claude integration

State of play in one line: the copilot is live and gated waiting on rollout decisions, and the Bid Quoter and diagram counter are in progress, both unblocked by more real bid packages.
